steps.title: Contrast impracticality with dominance
steps.content: The first sentence says envelope-shaped paper bags were impractical. The next sentence says they dominated the market because they were the only paper bags that could be mass-produced. The transition must signal this unexpected contrast.

steps.title: Reject example and result signals
steps.content: Choice B would introduce an example, but market dominance is not an example of impracticality. Choice C wrongly makes dominance the expected result of being impractical. Choice D wrongly treats dominance as a restatement. The answer is A.
